page: Microsoft Azure F8amds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Famdsv7 server family. It is powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The configuration includes 8 physical cores and 8 vCPUs, providing a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Additionally, it features 945 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, running under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU metrics, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all placing in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average range. For machine learning tasks, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds and strong prompt processing speeds on small 135M parameter models, though performance drops to the bottom 25% when processing prompts for large 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F8amds_v7 offers a balanced profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated GPU acceleration. The combination of high-frequency AMD EPYC cores, a generous 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and substantial local storage makes it highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, caching layers like Redis, and lightweight language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure F8amds_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 8 physical cores, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong performance in small-scale language model inference. While its multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks align with mid-range averages, its high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core and Premium Storage capability make it a highly capable option for memory-bound workloads. The F8amds_v7 is well-suited for database operations, caching systems, and memory-intensive enterprise applications that benefit from high-frequency single-core execution and substantial local disk space without requiring GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F8amds_v7 is an 8-vCPU memory-intensive virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, 64 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage, optimized for database and memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F8amds_v7 is an 8-vCPU memory-intensive virtual machine with 64 GB RAM and 945 GB local storage, powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC CPU.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-intensive virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and substantial local storage for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
Includes 945 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Best suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-intensive workloads
